name: ETLAS-2 found no cognition difference and more adverse events with daily tadalafil in CSVD
statement: In ETLAS-2, daily tadalafil 20 mg for 3 months in older CSVD patients did not differ from placebo on cognition, mental well-being, or blood pressure, while adverse events were reported more often with tadalafil and adherence was lower.
population_or_context: Older CSVD patients with prior stroke or transient ischemic attack; indirect for healthy men age 30 to 50.
quantitative_note: Adverse events occurred in 76% with tadalafil versus 36% with placebo; no differences were observed in cognition, mental well-being, or blood pressure in the PubMed abstract.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports no differences in cognition, mental well-being, or blood pressure and higher adverse-event frequency with tadalafil.

caveats: The trial population was older and clinically affected by CSVD.
The study does not test healthy-adult cognitive enhancement.
The dose, 20 mg daily, is higher than common low-dose daily ED/BPH tadalafil regimens.
population_profile: CSVD patients with previous stroke or transient ischemic attack; mean age 68.0 years.
endpoint_measure: Secondary cognition, mental well-being, blood pressure, adherence, and adverse-event outcomes.
effect_estimate: No differences observed in cognition, mental well-being, or blood pressure; adverse events 76% tadalafil versus 36% placebo.
safety_findings: Higher adverse-event frequency and lower adherence in the tadalafil group; this safety signal argues against benefit-only off-label framing.
directness_to_target_population: Indirect to healthy men 30-50 because the participants were older patients with CSVD and prior stroke/TIA.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Phase II feasibility-primary trial; secondary cognitive outcomes; indirect population and higher-dose daily exposure.
